facts.text: Law is the system of rules a society uses to regulate behavior, resolve disputes, and define rights and responsibilities.
The Code of Hammurabi, from ancient Babylon, is one of the earliest surviving written legal codes.
Roman law strongly influenced many modern legal traditions.
The Magna Carta of 1215 became a foundational symbol of limited government and the rule of law.
Common law developed in England through judicial decisions and precedent rather than only written codes.
Civil law systems, common in much of Europe and Latin America, rely more heavily on comprehensive written codes.
The idea of 'innocent until proven guilty' is a cornerstone of many justice systems.
Trial by jury allows ordinary citizens to help decide the outcome of legal cases in some countries.
The U.S. Supreme Court is the highest court in the United States and decides constitutional questions with national significance.
Miranda warnings in the United States come from the 1966 Supreme Court case Miranda v. Arizona.
Lawyers may work in criminal law, civil litigation, contracts, constitutional law, family law, intellectual property, and many other fields.
The scales of justice symbolize fairness and balanced judgment.
The phrase 'rule of law' means that no one, including leaders, is supposed to be above the law.
Courts rely on evidence, procedure, and standards of proof to reach decisions rather than personal opinion alone.
Justice systems are constantly debated and reformed as societies reconsider rights, punishment, policing, and equality before the law.
The Magna Carta of 1215 became an enduring symbol of limits on royal power and of legal rights.
Its legacy influenced constitutional ideas far beyond medieval England.
Magna Carta is one of the most important topics associated with Law & Justice.
Books, documentaries, and classrooms that cover Law & Justice frequently highlight Magna Carta.
Learning about Magna Carta helps explain a major part of Law & Justice.
For many readers, Magna Carta stands out as one of the defining subjects in Law & Justice.
The story of Magna Carta is closely tied to the broader themes of Law & Justice.
Magna Carta is often used to introduce students to the history and significance of Law & Justice.
Many surveys of Law & Justice return again and again to the importance of Magna Carta.
From public memory to scholarship, Magna Carta plays a notable role in how people understand Law & Justice.
One reason Law & Justice remains so compelling is the lasting significance of Magna Carta.
The legacy of Magna Carta continues to shape how Law & Justice is discussed today.
The U.S. Constitution established the framework of the federal government and separation of powers.
It is one of the most influential legal documents in modern history.

The legacy of United States Constitution continues to shape how Law & Justice is discussed today.
The Bill of Rights added the first ten amendments to the U.S. Constitution.
These amendments protect core civil liberties such as speech, religion, and due process.

Marbury v. Madison helped establish the principle of judicial review in the United States.
The case gave the Supreme Court lasting power to strike down unconstitutional laws.

Brown v. Board of Education declared racial segregation in public schools unconstitutional.
It became one of the central legal victories of the civil-rights movement.

Obergefell v. Hodges recognized a constitutional right to same-sex marriage in the United States.
The ruling became a landmark in modern equal-protection and civil-rights law.

Magna Carta became one of the most famous documents in the history of law and constitutional government.
It is often cited when discussing limits on power and the rule of law.
Magna Carta is one of the first topics that comes up when people explore Law & Justice.
Learning about Magna Carta gives a stronger feel for the depth and variety inside Law & Justice.
Books, documentaries, and reference guides on Law & Justice often highlight Magna Carta.
Magna Carta remains one of the most widely recognized examples connected with Law & Justice.
For many readers, Magna Carta is a memorable entry point into Law & Justice.
The story of Magna Carta helps explain why Law & Justice stays so interesting to learn about.
Magna Carta is frequently mentioned in discussions, lessons, and articles about Law & Justice.
One reason Law & Justice feels so rich is that it includes major touchpoints like Magna Carta.
Readers interested in Law & Justice often come across Magna Carta very quickly.
Magna Carta still stands out as a classic reference point within Law & Justice.

Separation of powers divides government authority among branches so no single part controls everything.
